settings.heading:
The healthcare system wasn't built with you in mind.
[accent]For Her Throne was.[/accent]
settings.subheading: Formulas selected for the health disparities Black women face — framed in NIH and CDC data, not generic wellness copy.
settings.social_proof: Chosen from the data. Built for you.

settings.heading: Black women's health has been systemically overlooked.
settings.subheading: These aren't editorial claims. These are published findings from NIH and CDC research.
settings.context_note: For Her Throne was built in direct response to this data. Every ingredient is chosen because of these disparities.

settings.description: Black women with PCOS face significant diagnostic delays and dismissal

settings.heading: Every ingredient chosen for a reason. Your reason.
settings.intro: Our formulas aren't adapted from generic supplements. Every ingredient is selected specifically because of documented disparities in Black women's hormonal health.

settings.name: Black Cohosh
settings.benefit: Supports hormonal comfort and menopausal transition
settings.relevance: Black women experience perimenopause on average 8 years earlier — Black Cohosh addresses that earlier timeline directly.

settings.name: Chasteberry (Vitex)
settings.benefit: Supports cycle regularity and hormonal balance
settings.relevance: PCOS and cycle irregularity disproportionately go undiagnosed in Black women. Vitex supports the cycle regulation they deserve.

settings.name: Dong Quai
settings.benefit: Traditional menstrual health and comfort support
settings.relevance: Used for centuries across healing traditions — largely absent from mainstream US supplement lines.

settings.name: Red Clover
settings.benefit: Phytoestrogen source for hormonal equilibrium support
settings.relevance: Rich in isoflavones that support the hormonal balance disrupted by early perimenopause.

settings.name: Sage Leaf
settings.benefit: Supports comfort during temperature regulation changes
settings.relevance: Hot flashes and night sweats begin earlier and more severely in Black women. Sage Leaf targets these experiences.

settings.name: Wild Yam
settings.benefit: Natural precursor support for hormonal pathways
settings.relevance: A natural source of diosgenin — supporting the adrenal and reproductive hormonal pathways.

settings.name: Licorice Root
settings.relevance: Chronic stress disrupts hormonal health. Black women carry a disproportionate allostatic load — Licorice Root supports adrenal resilience.
settings.contraindication: Consult a healthcare provider if you have high blood pressure or take blood pressure medication.

settings.name: Red Raspberry Leaf
settings.benefit: Menstrual comfort and pelvic wellness support
settings.relevance: A generational herb used across cultures for menstrual wellness, now at a therapeutic dose.

settings.heading: The community is building its own evidence.
settings.subheading: Real women sharing their Daily Reign journey — unscripted, always their own words.

settings.heading: Get the studies behind the formula.
settings.subheading: We send the NIH and CDC research on Black women's hormonal health directly to your inbox — plus early access to formulation updates.

settings.heading: For women who've been overlooked long enough.
